VIDEO OF A NON-SUPERCELLULAR, LANDSPOUT TORNADO WAS PROVIDED BY A VIEWER TO A LOCAL BROADCAST STATION ALONG STATE HIGHWAY 87 NEAR THE BUSH HIGHWAY JUNCTION. UPON FURTHER ANALYSIS OF RADAR AND VIDEO EVIDENCE, THIS LANDSPOUT FORMED ALONG A SOUTHWESTWARD MOVING OUTFLOW BOUNDARY AT A CONSIDERABLE DISTANCE FROM THE PARENT STORM. RADAR ANALYSIS DETERMINED THE VERTICAL STRETCHING OF BAROCLINICALLY GENERATED SURFACE VORTICITY WAS ENHANCED BY INCIPIENT UPDRAFTS ALONG AND IMMEDIATELY BEHIND THE ADVANCING OUTFLOW BOUNDARY, WHICH LIKELY CONTRIBUTED TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE LANDSPOUT. THUS, FORMATION OF THIS LANDSPOUT TORNADO ASSUMED PROCESSES ASSOCIATED WITH BOTH LANDSPOUTS AND GUSTNADOES, BUT DUE TO THE CONTINUOUS VERTICAL EXTENT OF THE ROTATING VORTEX EXTENDING UP TO THE CLOUD BASE CLEARLY SEEN IN VIDEO EVIDENCE, CONFIDENCE IS HIGH IN THIS CLASSIFICATION. THE LANDSPOUT TORNADO APPEARS TO HAVE REMAINED OVER OPEN DESERT TO THE EAST OF HIGHWAY 87.

A FLASH FLOOD CONTAINING A LOT OF SEDIMENT SPILLED OVER HIGHWAY 188 NEAR MILEPOST 253 AND INTO ROOSEVELT LAKE. THIS CAUSED THE HIGHWAY TO BE CLOSED IN ORDER FOR HEAVY EQUIPMENT TO REMOVE THE SEDIMENT AND BOULDERS AND REPAIR THE GUARD RAILS.

*** 2 FATAL, 3 INJ *** [DELAYED REPORT] A FLASH FLOOD WITH DEBRIS COMING DOWN ALDER CREEK ALONG THE NORTH SHORE OF APACHE LAKE CAUSED AN INOPERABLE BOAT WITH FIVE ADULTS ONBOARD TRYING TO ESCAPE THE AREA TO CAPSIZE. THE OTHER OPERABLE BOAT RESCUED THREE OF THE FIVE ADULTS OUT OF THE WATER. MARICOPA COUNTY S.O. RECOVERED A 41 YEAR OLD FEMALE WHO DROWNED, BUT A 52 YEAR OLD MALE IS STILL MISSING. THE PRIMARY CAUSE OF THE FLOOD WAS 1-2 INCHES OF RAIN THAT FELL WITHIN 20-30 MINUTES OVER THE HEADWATERS OF THE ALDER CREEK BASIN LOCATED WITHIN THE BUSH FIRE BURN SCAR. TIME OF IMPACT BASED ON S.O. AND MEDIA REPORTS.
